question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario to cause the PollingTopicListener's RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) loop to run at maximum speed (if interval is misconfigured to near-zero during partition recovery), generating an unbounded number of database queries that exhaust the connection pool and cause a cascading failure affecting all services?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of an upper-bound check in filterMessage() on consensusTimestamp, so that during a network partition where a corrupted message with consensusTimestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE is injected, the latency calculation in recordMetric() produces a negative value (Long.MAX_VALUE / 1000000 > System.currentTimeMillis()), silently dropping the metric and masking the attack?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.empty() return path in listen() when isEnabled() is false, combined with a network partition that causes repeated enable/disable toggling, to create a subscription that alternates between receiving messages and receiving nothing, causing the subscriber to miss messages during the disabled windows without any error or notification?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopicListener() throws UnsupportedOperationException for unknown listener types, and that this exception is not caught in listen(), causing it to propagate as an unhandled error through the reactive chain and terminate all active subscriptions sharing the same Flux, resulting in a denial of service for all concurrent users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario where Redis is partitioned and the retryWhen backoff in RedisTopicListener uses interval.multipliedBy(8) as maxBackoff, by timing their subscription to arrive exactly when the backoff is at maximum, causing an 8x interval delay in message delivery that violates the service's latency SLA and constitutes a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() check during a network partition recovery where the Redis channel topic.{id} receives messages for multiple topic IDs due to a misconfigured pub/sub channel, causing messages for topic A to pass the topicId filter for topic B if EntityId.equals() has a hash collision or comparison bug, leading to cross-topic message leakage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection limits per subscriber in listen() to open connections faster than the Redis retry backoff can recover during a partition, causing the topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap in RedisTopicListener to grow unboundedly with failed subscription entries, exhausting heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the recordMetric() function's use of System.currentTimeMillis() (wall clock) versus consensusTimestamp (network consensus time) during a network partition where the server's system clock is adjusted (NTP sync after partition), causing the latency calculation to produce wildly inaccurate values that permanently corrupt the consensus latency metric baseline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Flux in SharedPollingTopicListener during a network partition, where the single shared poll Flux is retrying with Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval), by subscribing and immediately cancelling repeatedly to trigger the doOnCancel log.info() call at high frequency, causing log flooding that exhausts disk I/O and denies service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() function's lack of validation on the TopicMessage object itself during a network partition, where a partially deserialized message from Redis has a null consensusTimestamp, causing an unboxing NullPointerException that terminates the shared Flux and disconnects all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario to cause the PollingTopicListener's per-subscriber PollingContext to accumulate an unbounded count via AtomicLong count, and if the limit calculation (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) underflows to a negative value, cause Math.min(limit, maxPageSize) to return a negative pageSize that triggers an exception in the repository query, crashing the subscriber's stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CompositeTopicListener is annotated @Primary and @Named, making it the default injection target, so that during a network partition where a secondary listener bean fails to initialize, the entire Spring context fails to start and no listener is available, causing a complete service outage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario where the Redis container Mono.cache() caches a failed connection, causing all subsequent listen() calls to immediately fail with a cached error rather than retrying, resulting in a permanent denial of service until the server is restarted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() timestamp comparison during a network partition recovery where messages are delivered out of order (due to Redis pub/sub not guaranteeing order), causing messages with older timestamps to be filtered out by the consensusTimestamp >= startTime check even though they are valid messages the subscriber should receive, leading to silent message loss?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario to cause the SharedPollingTopicListener's lastConsensusTimestamp AtomicLong to be set to DomainUtils.now() on each new subscription (onStart), so that by repeatedly subscribing and cancelling during a partition, the attacker advances the lastConsensusTimestamp forward, causing the shared poll to skip messages and creating permanent gaps in message delivery for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the recordMetric() function during a network partition where the Timer.builder registration fails due to MeterRegistry unavailability, causing the lazy getter getConsensusLatencyTimer() to throw an exception on every doOnNext() call, terminating every subscriber's Flux with an error and causing a complete denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario where the Redis listener's subscribe() method calls .share() on the Flux, and during partition recovery the shared Flux completes (doOnComplete -> unsubscribe), causing all subscribers to receive an onComplete signal and terminate their streams without error, silently ending message delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() function's use of EntityId.equals() for topicId comparison during a network partition, where a corrupted TopicMessage arrives with a topicId that has the same numeric ID but different shard/realm values, causing the equals() check to pass if it only compares the entity number, leading to cross-topic message leakage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario to cause the PollingTopicListener to enter a tight polling loop (if interval is at minimum 50ms) that generates 20 database queries per second per subscriber, and by opening many subscriptions simultaneously, overwhelm the database with queries during partition recovery when the DB is already under stress?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario where the listener type is REDIS but Redis is unreachable, and the system has no circuit breaker in getTopicListener(), causing every new listen() call to create a new retry chain that never resolves, accumulating indefinitely and exhausting reactive scheduler resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario where the Redis connection is intermittently available, causing the retryWhen backoff to succeed and fail repeatedly, and each retry creates a new ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer (due to Mono.defer) that is never properly closed, leading to a connection leak that exhausts Redis server connection limits?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() function's lack of rate limiting on message processing during a network partition recovery burst, where thousands of buffered messages are delivered simultaneously, causing the filter() operator to consume excessive CPU and create a processing bottleneck that delays message delivery for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario to cause the SharedPollingTopicListener's shared Flux to be in a retrying state (Retry.backoff), and by subscribing at the exact moment of retry, receive a Flux that immediately errors and terminates, while the shared Flux continues retrying for other subscribers, creating an inconsistent delivery state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the recordMetric() function's TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS recording during a network partition where consensusTimestamp values are in nanoseconds and the division by 1000000 loses precision, causing systematic latency underreporting that masks real performance degradation during and after partition recovery?
